report_name: ShinyHunters Threatens to Leak Cisco Data, Claims Breach of Salesforce and AWS

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A data extortion group that publicly threatened to leak sensitive data allegedly stolen from Cisco. The group claimed to have compromised Cisco's Salesforce and AWS environments through social engineering and other vectors.
threat_actors.goals: To extort money from Cisco by threatening to leak sensitive stolen data, including Salesforce records, source code, and PII.

threat_actors.name: UNC6040
threat_actors.description: A threat actor alias linked to a previously disclosed vishing campaign targeting Cisco employees. ShinyHunters referenced this actor, suggesting the current breach leveraged similar social engineering TTPs for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: Used in a vishing (voice phishing) campaign to trick a Cisco employee into providing credentials or approving a multi-factor authentication (MFA) push notification, which served as the initial intrusion vector.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Compromised employee credentials obtained through vishing were used to pivot and gain access to Cisco's cloud environments, including Salesforce and AWS.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: Used to exfiltrate over three million records from Cisco's compromised Salesforce environment.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage Object
attack_patterns.description: Used to exfiltrate data, including source code and other internal corporate files, from Cisco's AWS S3 buckets.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely generated multiple MFA push notifications to induce MFA fatigue or trick the targeted employee into approving a prompt as part of the vishing attack.

attack_patterns.name: Code Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor allegedly accessed and exfiltrated proprietary source code from Cisco's GitHub repositories.

tools.name: AWS Command Line Interface
tools.description: Potentially used by attackers to programmatically access and exfiltrate data from Cisco's compromised AWS environment after gaining access with stolen credentials.

tools.name: Salesforce CLI
tools.description: Potentially used by attackers to programmatically query and exfiltrate records from Cisco's compromised Salesforce environment after gaining access with stolen credentials.

identities.name: Cisco
identities.description: A multinational networking and security technology company targeted by the ShinyHunters extortion group. The group claims to have breached Cisco's internal cloud environments and exfiltrated millions of records and source code.

infrastructure.name: Cisco Salesforce Environment
infrastructure.description: Cisco's Salesforce CRM environment, which was allegedly compromised by ShinyHunters to exfiltrate over three million records containing customer data and PII.

infrastructure.name: Cisco AWS Environment
infrastructure.description: Cisco's Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud environment, which was allegedly compromised by ShinyHunters to access and exfiltrate source code from GitHub repositories and other internal files.

indicators.name: Suspicious AWS CLI Usage for Data Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious AWS CLI Usage for Data Exfiltration
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e4f-4b2a-9c1d-6a3b2c1d0e9f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the AWS CLI to copy or sync S3 bucket contents, which could indicate data exfiltration by an attacker who has compromised credentials.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/data-breaches/shinyhunters-issues-final-warning-to-cisco-over-alleged-data-theft
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\aws.exe'
  selection_cli:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 's3 cp'
      - 's3 sync'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or DevOps activity involving data transfer from S3 buckets.
indicators.description: Detects the use of the AWS command-line tool to copy or synchronize S3 buckets, a TTP that could be used for mass data exfiltration from a compromised AWS environment.

indicators.name: Suspicious Salesforce CLI Data Query
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Salesforce CLI Data Query
id: f8a7b6c5-d4e3-4f2a-8b1c-9d0e8f7a6b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the Salesforce CLI (sfdx) to execute a SOQL query, which could indicate an attacker attempting to exfiltrate data from a compromised Salesforce environment.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/data-breaches/shinyhunters-issues-final-warning-to-cisco-over-alleged-data-theft
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1213.002
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\sfdx.exe'
  selection_cli:
    CommandLine|contains: 'force:data:soql:query'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or developer activity involving data queries against Salesforce.
indicators.description: Detects the use of the Salesforce command-line tool to perform a SOQL query, a TTP that could be used to exfiltrate records from a compromised Salesforce environment.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious AWS CLI Usage for Data Exfiltration
